title: Farmhouse Table Made with 7 boards
summary: This farmhouse dining table is made with just seven 5/4″ thick, wide planks and chunky turned legs for an authentic, farmhouse look.
description: This farmhouse dining table is made with just seven 5/4″ thick, wide planks and chunky turned legs for an authentic, farmhouse look. These plans can be customized to make a custom-sized table that is just right for your dining needs.

steps.title: Cut wood
steps.body: Cut the end and side apron pieces.

steps.title: Route apron pieces (optional)
steps.body: Cut a groove into apron pieces 2" from the edge with a router

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Use Kreg Jig to drill pocket holes at both ends of each apron piece.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the long edges of aprons. These will be for attaching the table top.

steps.title: Assemble table frame
steps.body: Using 2-1/2" pocket screws and wood glue, assemble the table frame.

steps.title: Assemble table top
steps.body: Use wood glue and pocket screws to make table top with the five 8" wide planks.

steps.title: Sand and attach table top
steps.body: Use a belt sander or orbital sander to smooth table top. Attach table top to table from with wood glue and pocket screws.

steps.title: Finish!
steps.body: Paint or stain table as desired!
